mnemonics.title: Agile Values Memory Aid
mnemonics.content: Remember the 4 values as: 'People & Collaboration > Processes' - Focus on individuals first, then working software, then customer partnership, then embrace change.

mnemonics.title: Scrum Roles - PSM
mnemonics.content: P-S-M: Product Owner (Prioritizes), Scrum Master (Serves), Team Members (Builds) - Think 'P-S-M: People Serve Millions'

acronyms.expansion: Independent, Negotiable, Valuable, Estimable, Small, Testable
acronyms.usage: Quality criteria for writing effective User Stories
In Kanban, limiting WIP helps reduce bottlenecks and improve flow
Criteria that must be met for a user story to be considered complete
The smallest version of a product that can be released to validate assumptions
Criteria for setting effective Sprint goals and user stories

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a circular conveyor belt - items enter (backlog), move through stages (Sprint), and exit as finished product (done). The belt never stops.
Think of a restaurant kitchen display - orders come in (To Do), are being prepared (In Progress), and served (Done). Only a few dishes can be cooked at once.
Picture a mountain climber descending - the line shows remaining height (work) vs time. If line is above diagonal, they're behind schedule.

analogies.analogy: Waterfall is like building a house with all blueprints fixed before construction. Agile is like building with modular prefabricated sections that can be modified as you live in parts of the house.
A Sprint is like a 2-week 'film shoot' in a movie production. Each sprint produces a 'scene' (increment) that could be previewed, rather than waiting for the entire movie.
Think of a restaurant menu - the Product Owner decides what dishes (features) to offer and in what order, based on customer preferences.
It's like a quick team huddle before a sports game - each player shares status, plans, and any blocks in under 15 seconds each.

analogies.analogue: The Scrum Master is like a supportive coach who ensures the team has what they need to win, rather than directing them from the sidelines.
